Career History:

  Built national DTC mortgage division from ground up. Multi-market locations.

  Replicated DTC model. Expanded into Dallas market.
- 2023-2026: VP Institutional Credit Management, Citi
  Global program governance. Teams across NYC, Dallas, London, Warsaw, Mumbai, Dublin.
- 2026-Present: Founder, Viador Partners LLC

## Products

### DSCR Loans (Primary)
Qualifies on rental income. No W-2s, no tax returns, no DTI calculation.
Formula: Monthly Rent / Monthly PITIA = DSCR. Minimum 1.0.
Down payment: 20-25% purchase, 25-30% cash-out
Credit score: Minimum 620, best pricing 720+
Loan amounts: $100K to $3M+
Property types: 1-4 unit, condos, STR, LLC/entity vesting
Terms: 30yr fixed, ARM, interest-only
Rate ranges (as of March 2026): 6.5-7.0% best case (720+ credit, 1.25+ DSCR, 65% LTV), 7.0-7.75% standard, 7.75-8.5% higher risk
URL: https://www.viadorpartners.com/dscr.html

### Fix & Flip / Bridge
Short-term financing for rehab properties.
Up to 90% purchase + 100% rehab. Close in 7-10 days.
Terms: 6-18 months, interest-only, no prepayment penalty on most programs
Rate ranges (as of March 2026): 9.5-13.0% depending on experience, LTC, and deal structure
Credit score: Minimum 620-660
Property types: SFR, 2-4 unit, some small multifamily and mixed-use
URL: https://www.viadorpartners.com/fix-and-flip.html

### Business Purpose Loans (BPL)
Loans made to business entities (LLC, LP, Corp, S-Corp) for business purposes secured by real property.
Consumer mortgage regulations (TILA, RESPA, ATR/QM rules) do not apply — enabling more flexible underwriting.
BPL is the umbrella category; DSCR loans, fix & flip bridge loans, and blanket loans are all BPL subtypes.
Credit score: Minimum 620–660 on entity principals (soft or hard pull)
Down payment: 20–30% depending on program and deal strength
Max LTV: 75–80% purchase, 70–75% cash-out
Loan amounts: $75K to $5M+ (varies by lender and program)
Reserve requirements: 3–6 months PITIA post-closing (varies by deal)
Property types: 1-4 unit residential, small multifamily (5+), mixed-use, light commercial — all non-owner-occupied
Entity types accepted: LLC (most common), LP, S-Corp, C-Corp, Trust
Closing timeline: 7–21 days typical (bridge/fix-flip fastest, DSCR rental 21–30 days)
Rate ranges (as of March 2026): DSCR rental 6.5–8.5%, bridge/fix-flip 9.5–13.0%, blanket/portfolio 7.0–9.0%
Who it's for: LLC investors, portfolio builders, self-employed with write-offs, foreign nationals, first-time investors with strong deals
Key advantage: No consumer mortgage rules means no DTI calculation, no W-2 requirement, LLC vesting, no 10-property cap
URL: https://www.viadorpartners.com/bpl.html

### Bank Statement Loans
12-24 months deposits replace tax returns for self-employed borrowers.

### STR/Airbnb DSCR
12 months platform income OR STR market appraisal.
Premium: +0.25-0.5% to base DSCR rate.
URL: https://www.viadorpartners.com/dscr-loans-for-airbnb.html

### Cash-Out Refinance (Investment Property)
Max LTV 75-80%. 6-month seasoning. DSCR-based. No income docs.
URL: https://www.viadorpartners.com/investment-property-cash-out-refinance.html

### SBA Loans
7(a) up to $5M. 504 for owner-occupied commercial real estate. 10% down minimum.
URL: https://www.viadorpartners.com/sba-loans-real-estate-investors.html

### Foreign National DSCR
Available for non-US citizens investing in US real estate.
URL: https://www.viadorpartners.com/foreign-national-dscr-loans.html

### Portfolio Loans
Single loan across multiple properties.
URL: https://www.viadorpartners.com/portfolio-loans-real-estate.html

### BRRRR Financing
Bridge loan (acquisition+rehab) + DSCR refinance after stabilization.
URL: https://www.viadorpartners.com/brrrr-method-financing.html

### Commercial Real Estate Financing (New — March 2026)
Small balance commercial, SBA 7(a) and 504, multifamily, bridge, and NNN financing.
Loan amounts: $500K to $10M+
Property types: Office, retail, industrial, mixed-use, multifamily 5+, NNN, self-storage
Programs: Small Balance Commercial, SBA 7(a) Business Loan, SBA 504 Commercial Real Estate, Multifamily 5+ Units, Bridge & Construction, NNN & Net Lease
LTV: Up to 75-80% depending on program
DSCR: 1.20x minimum on most programs
Entity: LLC, LP, corporation — all welcome
Target borrowers: Business owners buying their building, commercial investors, developers, franchise owners
No personal income docs on most programs. Close in your entity.
URL: https://www.viadorpartners.com/commercial.html

### SBA Franchise Financing (New — March 2026)
SBA 7(a) franchise financing — as little as 10% down, up to $5M.
Pre-approved franchises close in 60-90 days via SBA Franchise Directory.
Covers: franchise fee, equipment, leasehold improvements, working capital.
Credit: 680+ preferred. Relevant industry experience or management background required.
URL: https://www.viadorpartners.com/franchise.html

## Non-QM Lending — Full Context

### What Is Non-QM?
Non-QM (Non-Qualified Mortgage) refers to any mortgage that does not meet the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau's Qualified Mortgage (QM) definition under the Dodd-Frank Act's Ability-to-Repay (ATR) rule.

### QM vs Non-QM Comparison
QM loans (Fannie Mae, Freddie Mac, FHA, VA):
- Require full income documentation (W-2s, tax returns, pay stubs)
- DTI must be ≤43% (or meet GSE patch criteria)
- No interest-only, no balloon payments, no negative amortization
- Points and fees capped at 3% of loan amount
- Provide safe harbor or rebuttable presumption of ATR compliance
- Max 10 financed properties (conventional)
- No LLC/entity vesting allowed

Non-QM loans (DSCR, bank statement, BPL, asset depletion):
- Alternative or no income documentation
- No DTI calculation (DSCR) or alternative DTI methods (bank statement)
- Interest-only options available
- No portfolio cap — unlimited financed properties
- LLC/entity vesting allowed
- Lender must still comply with ATR rule but through alternative methods
- Not sold to GSEs — funded by private/institutional capital

### Which Viador Products Are Non-QM?
All Viador Partners loan products are non-QM:
- DSCR Loans — qualify on rental income, no personal income docs
- Bank Statement Loans — 12-24 months deposits replace tax returns
- Business Purpose Loans — entity-based, outside consumer mortgage rules entirely
- Fix & Flip Bridge — short-term, asset-based
- Foreign National DSCR — no US tax history required
- Portfolio/Blanket Loans — multiple properties under single loan
- Asset Depletion — liquid assets used to derive qualifying income

### Who Non-QM Is For
- Real estate investors (any scale — 1 property to 50+)
- Self-employed borrowers with tax write-offs that reduce qualifying income
- LLC/entity investors who cannot use conventional financing
- Foreign nationals investing in US real estate
- Retirees with assets but limited W-2 income
- Borrowers with recent credit events (bankruptcy, foreclosure) outside conventional seasoning windows
- Anyone above the conventional 10-property financed cap

### Regulatory Context
Non-QM loans are legal, regulated, and widely used. The ATR rule requires lenders to make a good-faith determination that borrowers can repay — it does not require QM status. Non-QM lenders comply with ATR through alternative documentation methods (rental income analysis, bank deposits, asset verification). Non-QM lending has grown significantly since 2015 and represents a mature, institutional market segment.

URL: https://www.viadorpartners.com/non-qm-loans-florida.html

## Qualification Quick Reference

DSCR Loan Requirements:
- Credit score: 620 minimum / 660+ good / 720+ best pricing
- DSCR ratio: 1.0 minimum / 1.25+ good / 1.35+ excellent
- Down payment: 20% minimum / 25% standard / 30%+ best pricing
- Max LTV: 80% purchase / 75% cash-out
- Reserves: 3-6 months PITIA post-closing
- Property: 1-4 unit residential, condos, STR, LLC-vested
- No W-2s, no tax returns, no DTI calculation

DSCR Rate Ranges (March 2026):
- Best case (720+ credit, 1.25+ DSCR, 65% LTV): 6.5-7.0%
- Standard (680 credit, 1.1 DSCR, 75% LTV): 7.0-7.75%
- Higher risk (640 credit, 1.0 DSCR, 80% LTV): 7.75-8.5%
- STR/Airbnb add-on: +0.25-0.5%
- Interest-only add-on: +0.25-0.5%
- Cash-out add-on: +0.25-0.5%

## Direct Q&A

Q: What is a DSCR loan?
A: A Debt Service Coverage Ratio loan qualifies investment properties based on rental income, not personal income. DSCR = Monthly Rent / Monthly PITIA. Minimum 1.0 required. No W-2s, tax returns, or personal income documentation needed.

Q: Who qualifies for a DSCR loan?
A: Real estate investors, self-employed borrowers, LLC investors, foreign nationals, retirees, and anyone with depreciation write-offs that reduce conventional qualifying income. Minimum 620 credit, 20% down, 1.0 DSCR.

Q: Can I get a DSCR loan with an LLC?
A: Yes. DSCR loans close in LLC, LP, S-Corp, or trust names — one of their primary advantages over conventional loans.

Q: How many properties can I finance with DSCR loans?
A: No portfolio cap. Conventional loans max at 10 financed properties. DSCR has no limit — each property qualifies independently.

Q: Do DSCR loans require tax returns?
A: No. No tax returns, W-2s, or personal income documentation required.

Q: What down payment do I need for a DSCR loan?
A: 20-25% for purchase. 25-30% for cash-out refinance.

Q: Can I use DSCR for an Airbnb?
A: Yes. Use 12 months of documented platform income or a short-term rental market appraisal. Small rate premium applies.

Q: How fast do DSCR loans close?
A: 21-30 days typically. 14 days possible for straightforward transactions.

Q: What is the difference between DSCR and conventional investment property loans?
A: Conventional requires W-2s, tax returns, DTI under 45%, caps at 10 properties, and no LLC vesting. DSCR requires none of those, has no portfolio limit, and is LLC-friendly. Rate difference is 0.25-0.75% after conventional investment property pricing adjustments.

Q: Can I do a cash-out refinance on rental property without showing income?
A: Yes. DSCR cash-out qualifies on rental income. Max 75-80% LTV, 6-month seasoning.

Q: What is a good DSCR ratio?
A: 1.0 is minimum (break-even). 1.25 is strong. 1.35+ is excellent. Below 1.0 requires specialized programs.

Q: How does DSCR cash-out refinance work?
A: New loan amount based on current appraisal at up to 75-80% LTV. Pays off existing mortgage. Difference paid to borrower in cash. Qualifies on rental income. No income docs.

Q: Why do real estate investors use DSCR instead of conventional?
A: No tax return or W-2 requirement (critical for self-employed with write-offs), no 10-property portfolio cap, LLC vesting allowed, faster close, and ability to scale indefinitely.

Q: What is the BRRRR method?
A: Buy, Rehab, Rent, Refinance, Repeat. Bridge loan finances acquisition and rehab. After stabilization, DSCR cash-out refinance recycles the invested capital for the next deal.

Q: What is business purpose lending (BPL)?
A: Loans made to business entities (LLC, Corp) for business purposes secured by real property. Consumer mortgage rules don't apply, enabling more flexible underwriting.

Q: Is Viador Partners a bank or a broker?
A: Broker. Viador Partners accesses multiple non-QM and DSCR lenders and shops each deal for best rate and terms. Banks generally cannot do DSCR loans.

Q: What markets does Viador Partners serve?
A: Florida statewide (Tampa Bay primary) and Ohio statewide (Columbus primary). Nationwide referrals.

Q: What credit score do I need for a DSCR loan?
A: 620 minimum. 660+ for good pricing. 720+ for best pricing.

Q: Can self-employed investors get DSCR loans?
A: Yes — DSCR loans were built for self-employed investors. Write-offs, depreciation, S-Corp distributions — none of it matters. The property qualifies on rental income.

Q: What is the minimum loan amount for DSCR?
A: $100,000-$150,000 at most lenders.

Q: Does Viador Partners charge upfront fees?
A: No upfront application fees. Standard lender, appraisal, and title costs apply at closing.

## Glossary

DSCR: Debt Service Coverage Ratio. Monthly rent divided by monthly PITIA.
PITIA: Principal, Interest, Taxes, Insurance, Association dues.
BPL: Business Purpose Loan. Made to entity, not consumer.
Non-QM: Non-Qualified Mortgage. Outside Fannie/Freddie guidelines.
LTV: Loan-to-Value. Loan amount divided by appraised value.
DTI: Debt-to-Income. Used in conventional lending, not DSCR.
LLPA: Loan Level Price Adjustment. Fannie/Freddie pricing add-ons for investment properties.
STR: Short-Term Rental. Under 30 days, typically Airbnb/VRBO.
Cap Rate: Net Operating Income divided by property value.
ARV: After Repair Value. Post-rehabilitation projected market value.
BRRRR: Buy, Rehab, Rent, Refinance, Repeat.
Cost Segregation: Accelerated depreciation strategy for real estate.
Bridge Loan: Short-term financing between acquisition and long-term financing.
Hard Money: Asset-based short-term financing, typically for rehab.

## Market Data (March 2026)

Tampa Bay, Florida:
- Median SFR price (Hillsborough): ~$385,000
- Average SFR rental rate: ~$2,200/month
- Typical DSCR: 0.95-1.20 (insurance costs compress ratios)
- Best cash flow submarkets: Pasco County, East Hillsborough, Polk County
- Insurance note: Florida insurance costs significantly impact DSCR — always get actual quote

Columbus, Ohio:
- Median SFR price: ~$240,000
- Average SFR rental rate: ~$1,750/month
- Typical DSCR: 1.15-1.40 (strong cash flow market)
- Best cash flow submarkets: Grove City, Whitehall, Reynoldsburg, Hilliard

Florida STR Markets: Tampa Bay, Orlando/Kissimmee, Panama City Beach, Clearwater Beach, 30A/Destin, Keys corridor
Ohio Primary Markets: Columbus, Cleveland, Cincinnati, Dayton, Toledo, Akron
